citations.quote: Healthy Ones are able to accomplish many of their objectives because they maintain a balanced self-discipline. They work very hard and make good use of their time, but they also know when 'enough is enough' and it is time to rest or play.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Ones actualize themselves and remain healthy by allowing the spontaneous arising of their instinctive response to life, as in healthy Sevens. Ones discover that they can permit themselves to be affected by reality without needing to tense themselves against it.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: The keyword Ones need in order to heal is acceptance. This does not mean permissiveness; it means that if I really want to be in the service of good, I have to work with what is.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Deep down, Ones remember the essential quality of perfection. They know that, at a profound level, the universe is unfolding exactly as it must.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: "You are good or okay if you do what is right." "You are good or okay if you are loved by others and close to them." "You are good or okay if you do something valuable." "You are good or okay if you are true to yourself." "You are good or okay if you have thoroughly mastered something." "You are good or okay if you cover all the bases and do what is expected of you." "You are good or okay if you feel good and are getting what you want." "You are good or okay as long as you are strong and in control of your situation." "You are good or okay as long as everyone around you is good or okay."

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Ones struggle to be good, but they are never good enough for their own superegos. Why does your value depend on someone loving you, and how do you know if they do? Even if they don't, what has that got to do with you? Twos struggle to get closer to others but still feel unloved.

core_identity.what_reveals: A strong identification with being principled and morally upright.
core_identity.mechanism: An internalized belief that worth is tied to moral integrity.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore the possibility that worth is inherent and not solely based on moral actions.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to maintaining personal integrity and ethical standards.
core_identity.mechanism: A need to avoid feelings of being corrupt or defective by adhering strictly to personal values.
core_identity.growth_edge: Consider the flexibility of ethical standards and the value of compassion over rigid adherence.

core_identity.what_reveals: An internalized standard of perfection that guides self-perception.
core_identity.mechanism: The inner critic enforces a belief in the necessity of flawlessness.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate self-compassion and acceptance of imperfections as part of the human experience.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to integrity and personal responsibility.
core_identity.mechanism: A belief that worthiness is contingent upon adherence to high standards.
core_identity.growth_edge: Recognize the value of self-acceptance beyond perfection.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to integrity and ethical behavior.
core_identity.mechanism: A belief that maintaining high standards will prevent corruption.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore flexibility in standards to allow for human imperfection.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to moral integrity and ethical living.
core_identity.mechanism: The internalization of a strict moral code as a guide for behavior.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivating acceptance of imperfection in oneself and others.

core_identity.mechanism: A belief that worthiness is tied to adherence to high standards.
core_identity.growth_edge: Exploring the value of flexibility and self-compassion.

core_identity.what_reveals: A desire to be seen as reliable and principled.
core_identity.mechanism: Adherence to a personal code of conduct as a means of self-validation.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore flexibility in self-expectations to allow for personal growth.

core_identity.what_reveals: A strong identification with being a person who corrects wrongs.
core_identity.mechanism: The inner critic drives a need to reform and improve.
core_identity.growth_edge: Embrace the idea that imperfection is part of the human experience.

core_identity.what_reveals: A strong identification with being principled and purposeful.
core_identity.mechanism: A belief that one's value is contingent upon adherence to high standards.
core_identity.growth_edge: Consider the value of self-compassion and acceptance of human fallibility.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep-seated need to be seen as good and virtuous.
core_identity.mechanism: A reliance on external validation to affirm self-worth.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore intrinsic motivations beyond external approval.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to integrity and correctness.
core_identity.mechanism: A belief that worthiness is contingent upon adherence to high standards.
core_identity.growth_edge: Reflect on the flexibility of your standards and how they serve or hinder your well-being.

core_identity.what_reveals: A strong identification with moral integrity and a desire to be seen as good.
core_identity.mechanism: An internalized standard of perfection that guides self-worth.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore the possibility of self-acceptance beyond external validation.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to principles and ethical living.
core_identity.mechanism: A belief that worthiness is contingent upon adherence to rules.
core_identity.growth_edge: Consider the value of flexibility and compassion in self-assessment.

core_identity.what_reveals: An identification with being principled and responsible.
core_identity.mechanism: Adherence to high standards as a means to avoid feelings of defectiveness.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate self-compassion and acceptance of human flaws.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to integrity and ethical living.
core_identity.mechanism: A belief that worthiness is tied to moral correctness.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore the flexibility in moral perspectives to enhance personal growth.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to integrity and a desire to be seen as good.
core_identity.mechanism: The inner critic drives a need for self-improvement and adherence to high standards.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate self-compassion and recognize the inherent goodness within, beyond achievements.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to moral integrity and the pursuit of an ideal world.
core_identity.mechanism: Internalization of a strict moral code as a means to avoid feelings of defectiveness.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate acceptance of imperfections in self and others to foster compassion.

core_identity.mechanism: Internalization of a strict moral code as a means to gain approval and self-worth.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore the origins of your moral beliefs and consider how they align with your authentic self.

core_identity.what_reveals: An identification with being a person of integrity and high standards.
core_identity.mechanism: Adopting a rigid self-concept to avoid feelings of inadequacy.
core_identity.growth_edge: Allow yourself to embrace imperfections as part of the human experience.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to moral integrity and a strong sense of duty.
core_identity.mechanism: An internalized belief that worth is tied to ethical behavior and adherence to high standards.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate self-compassion and recognize that worth is inherent, not earned through perfection.

core_identity.what_reveals: A tendency to see the world in black and white terms, with a focus on right and wrong.
core_identity.mechanism: Cognitive rigidity stemming from a need for order and predictability.
core_identity.growth_edge: Embrace the complexity and nuance in situations, allowing for flexibility and understanding.

core_identity.what_reveals: An identity built around being the 'good' and 'responsible' one.
core_identity.mechanism: Identification with a role that demands high standards and self-control.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore the freedom in being authentic rather than perfect.

core_identity.what_reveals: An identification with being a reformer who seeks to improve the world.
core_identity.mechanism: Projection of internal standards onto external situations as a way to manage anxiety about imperfection.
core_identity.growth_edge: Practice accepting imperfections in self and others as part of the human experience.

core_identity.what_reveals: A persistent drive to improve oneself and the world.
core_identity.mechanism: Perfectionism fueled by an underlying fear of being flawed or corrupt.
core_identity.growth_edge: Embrace imperfections as part of the human experience.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to personal integrity and ethical living.
core_identity.mechanism: Perfectionistic standards as a measure of self-worth.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate self-compassion and acceptance of human flaws.

core_identity.what_reveals: A strong identification with being principled and ethical, often feeling a sense of duty to uphold moral standards.
core_identity.mechanism: The internalization of a strict superego that demands adherence to high ethical standards.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate self-compassion and recognize that imperfection is a part of being human.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep-seated fear of being corrupt or defective, driving a need to be seen as good and virtuous.
core_identity.mechanism: An internalized belief that worth is contingent upon moral integrity.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore the origins of this fear and practice self-acceptance beyond moral achievements.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to integrity and ethical standards.
core_identity.mechanism: A belief that worthiness is contingent upon moral behavior.
core_identity.growth_edge: Explore the value of flexibility and adaptability in moral judgments.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to moral integrity and personal ethics.
core_identity.mechanism: Internalization of a strict moral code.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate compassion for self and others to balance the inner critic.

core_identity.what_reveals: A strong identification with being morally upright and principled.
core_identity.mechanism: Internalization of a strict moral code as a means to avoid feelings of defectiveness.
core_identity.growth_edge: Cultivate self-compassion to balance the inner critic's harshness.

core_identity.what_reveals: A deep commitment to personal integrity and ethical behavior.
core_identity.mechanism: A belief that personal worth is contingent upon adherence to high standards.
core_identity.growth_edge: Recognize the inherent worth beyond achievements and moral perfection.
